_purpose: Explicit, auditable exemptions for Check 40 (scripts/check_local_paths.py), which forbids local absolute paths in this PUBLIC repo per AGENT.md's secret & PII hygiene rule.
_policy: RATCHET. Every entry below is PRE-EXISTING content that predates the check (added 2026-07-18, R59). None contains a username. The check is an ERROR for anything NOT listed here, so new leaks fail the gate while archived history is left alone. A `path_prefix` entry must ALSO pin `match` - a directory-wide exemption would hide the next real leak, which is exactly the failure this check exists to close.
_how_to_add: Only add an entry with a stated reason, and only when the path is genuinely non-identifying or is institutional documentation a colleague needs. If you are tempted to add a /Users/<name> path, fix the content instead.

entries.reason: R55 depth-round artifacts cite the read-only develop worktree they were audited against. Frozen round history; contains no username and no personal data. Left as-is rather than rewriting archived evidence - the round's citations are its provenance.
Institutional PIK path documenting where the shared R library lives - this is information a colleague following bootstrap NEEDS, not a local artifact. Non-identifying.
Pre-existing default path in the attribution checker (flagged during the R58 push as pre-existing and not introduced by that work). No username. Tracked separately as a cleanup item.
Comment recording the read-only worktree the self-test fixture was verified against. Provenance note; no username.
R55 measurement artifact recording the worktree it was computed from. No username.
